text: Initialize a new manuscript linked to C:\Users\smsakewe\Documents\GitHub\KnowledgeVault, use the proposal in vault\syntheses\Research proposal - noise-robust sign-restricted SVARs.md, and prepare a carefully revised manuscript writing plan and next tasks.
User asked to remove the first two template-era transparency milestones from the manuscript timeline without reverting the work performed in those milestones.

User corrected the Bonhomme-Robin summary in KnowledgeVault and asked to carefully analyze the updated notes, revise the manuscript plan, add detailed tasks for analytic derivation and simulation verification of the Bonhomme-Robin-style results, and insert adversarial review tasks to catch mistakes, undefined objects, logical gaps, and weak story points.
Complete the next open project task by finishing the stale M21 transparency cleanup for the already-closed M0005 BR verification plan milestone.

Continue to the next project task, M05: derive the corrected bivariate cumulant map from scratch for u_t = B(a,b) epsilon_t + eta_t, including all second-, third-, and fourth-order cumulant equations and nuisance noise cumulants.

Continue with M06: adversarially audit the bivariate cumulant derivation before using it, checking indices, cumulant definitions, normalization, missing pure or mixed moments, and whether any moment is incorrectly called clean.

Continue with M07: prove what the Bonhomme-Robin analogy does and does not justify by showing why full BR quasi-JADE does not directly apply for the bivariate L=K=2 SVAR and documenting the manuscript object as a BR-style profiled inversion.

Create a new derivation file that derives a Drautzburg-Wright-like higher-moment approach robust to additive noise, shows higher moments or cumulants can be written as moment conditions robust to Gaussian noise while second moments are not robust, and contrasts an incorrect no-noise sign-plus-higher-moment approach with a noise-robust higher-moment approach that sacrifices second-moment information.
